subproducts.name: Maps JavaScript API
subproducts.rationale: The claim centers on displaying a map with an address correlated to location data, which aligns with browser-based map rendering and address/location visualization documented for the Maps JavaScript API.

subproducts.name: Geocoding API
subproducts.rationale: The first terminal correlates a received address to location-based data, which is the core address-to-coordinate functionality covered by the Geocoding API.

subproducts.name: Places API
subproducts.rationale: If the dispatch workflow uses location-based data tied to addresses or destinations, Places API documentation can evidence how Google Maps Platform resolves and enriches location/place information.

subproducts.name: Directions API
subproducts.rationale: A dispatch system commonly transmits event and location data to support routing or assignment decisions, and Directions API docs cover route computation between locations.

subproducts.name: Maps SDK for Android
subproducts.rationale: The second terminal is a map display unit that automatically inputs location data and displays a map, which matches mobile map rendering and location display behavior documented in the Android SDK.

subproducts.name: Maps SDK for iOS
subproducts.rationale: The claim’s second terminal behavior also maps to iOS mobile map display and automatic location presentation features documented in the iOS SDK.
